question_text: A student sets up a circuit with a battery, a resistor, and measuring instruments. They want to measure both the current through the resistor and the voltage across it. Which statement correctly describes how these instruments should be connected?

options.label: A
options.text: The ammeter in parallel with the resistor and the voltmeter in series with it

options.label: B
options.text: The ammeter in series with the resistor and the voltmeter in parallel with it

options.label: C
options.text: Both the ammeter and voltmeter in series with the resistor

options.label: D
options.text: Both the ammeter and voltmeter in parallel with the resistor

explanation: An ammeter measures current and must be placed in series (in the main circuit path) so all the current flows through it. A voltmeter measures the potential difference and must be placed in parallel (across the component) to measure the voltage without disrupting the circuit.
